skill.name: error-recovery
skill.description: Strategies for handling subagent failures with retry logic and escalation patterns.
skill.summary: Strategies for handling subagent failures with retry logic and escalation patterns.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown examples and prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious behavior were found.

content.value_statement: Agent workflows can fail because of timeouts, unclear context, or task boundaries. This skill provides structured retry, escalation, and abort patterns for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Classifies subagent failures into transient, context, complexity, boundary, and fatal categories.
Provides retry guidance for malformed output, timeouts, and empty responses.
Shows how to add context after a failed agent attempt.
Explains when to split broad tasks into smaller subagent assignments.
Defines escalation and abort patterns for blocked or repeatedly failing tasks.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not run recovery logic automatically.
It assumes the host agent can launch subagents through a Task tool.
It does not verify whether generated subagent outputs are correct.
It does not include executable scripts, tests, or integrations.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Multi-Agent Development
use_cases.description: Recover implementation tasks that fail because of timeout, malformed output, or missing context.

use_cases.title: Handle Blocked Task Contracts
use_cases.description: Route boundary violations and contract changes to a resolver instead of retrying the same task.

use_cases.title: Create Failure Reports
use_cases.description: Stop repeated failures with a concise report that records attempts, reasons, and recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Classify a Failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for one failed subagent task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the error-recovery skill to classify this failed subagent output. Explain whether I should retry, add context, reduce scope, escalate, or abort.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Retry
prompt_templates.scenario: Retry after a timeout or malformed response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the error-recovery skill to create a retry plan for this task failure. Include the retry limit, added context, and stopping condition.

prompt_templates.title: Split a Complex Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Recover a task that failed because the scope was too broad.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the error-recovery skill to split this failed task into smaller subagent tasks. Keep each task independently completable and define recombination steps.

prompt_templates.title: Escalate or Abort
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced handling after multiple recovery attempts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the error-recovery skill to decide whether this blocked task should escalate or abort. Produce a failure report with attempts, evidence, and next recommendation.

output_examples.input: A subagent timed out twice and returned an empty response on the third attempt.
output_examples.output: The skill would classify this as a transient failure that has reached the retry limit. It would recommend stopping retries and writing a failure report.

output_examples.input: A subagent says the requirements are unclear and asks which file contains the configuration pattern.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend context enhancement. It would add the relevant files, the previous error, and the original task before retrying.

output_examples.input: A task returns blocked because changing a contract affects downstream dependents.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend escalation to a contract resolver instead of repeated implementation retries.

best_practices: Classify the failure before retrying so repeated attempts do not hide the real issue.
Preserve useful partial work and pass it into the next recovery step.
Record attempts, errors, and recovery decisions in shared task state.
anti_patterns: Do not retry the same prompt repeatedly after a boundary or contract failure.
Do not expand a failing task without adding specific context or examples.
Do not continue after three failed recovery strategies without a written report.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide and does not include executable command logic.

faq.question: Which tools does it expect?
faq.answer: It expects Read and Task capabilities, mainly for reviewing context and launching subagents.

faq.question: When should I use simple retry?
faq.answer: Use simple retry for timeouts, empty output, malformed output, or other transient failures.

faq.question: When should I escalate instead of retrying?
faq.answer: Escalate when a task is blocked by boundaries, contract changes, dependencies, or design issues.

faq.question: Can it guarantee recovery?
faq.answer: No. It helps choose a recovery path, but some tasks should abort with a clear report.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The concepts apply to Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that coordinate subagents.
